HEADING: Connect Zendesk Guide
INTRO: Add FastComments comments to your Zendesk Guide (Help Center) articles. Enter your Zendesk subdomain, then paste the generated snippet into your Help Center theme.
ERROR_INVALID_SUBDOMAIN: That does not look like a valid Zendesk subdomain. Enter just the subdomain, for example "mycompany" for mycompany.zendesk.com.
ERROR_SUBDOMAIN_TAKEN: That Zendesk subdomain is already connected to a different FastComments account.
ERROR_SAVE_FAILED: We could not save your Zendesk connection. Please try again.
HELP_SUBDOMAIN: The subdomain part of your Zendesk URL. For mycompany.zendesk.com, enter "mycompany".
HELP_HELP_CENTER_HOST: Only needed if your Help Center is served from a custom domain instead of the default zendesk.com address.
SSO_MODE_CLIENT_TRUSTED: Client-trusted (works on any plan, but spoofable)
SSO_HC_JWT_NOTE: Help Center JWT verifies each commenter with Zendesk's own signed token, so identities cannot be spoofed. It requires a Guide Enterprise or Suite Enterprise plan; on lower plans, commenters fall back to anonymous.
SSO_CLIENT_TRUSTED_WARNING: Client-trusted mode takes the commenter's identity straight from the browser without verifying it, so it can be spoofed. Use it only if you accept that risk. It works on any Zendesk plan.
SNIPPET_INSTRUCTIONS: In Zendesk Guide, open Customize design, then Edit code, and paste this block into templates/article_page.hbs where you want comments to appear.
DOCS_LINK: Read the full Zendesk installation guide
COPY_FAILED: Copy failed, select and copy manually
